prompt: A low-angle, hyper-realistic cinematic photograph. Thick, oppressive, blue-grey fog completely obscures the landscape of a jagged, wet shale mountain. Only the foreground is visible. A close-up on worn, mud-caked leather hiking boots on the slippery rock. A hand firmly grips a gnarled, dark wooden walking stick, pressing it into the shale ahead, testing the slope. The light is dramatic and moody, casting deep shadows. The composition is focused on the tension of the next cautious step downward. No text, no floating elements, no charts, 8k resolution.
metaphor: The fog obscures the global landscape while the hiker uses a walking stick to test the local slope, finding the downward direction one small step at a time.
